You Pardeep Bairagi , declare that, you have fully understood the questions asked to you as per this call and have furnished complete, true and accurate information after fully understanding the same. Sir/Mam' are you in agreement with the declaration that I have read out to you.
qa_matrix.captured_response: So, here I declare that all the information you have given is true, and we will use all this information to prepare your policy. In case there is any query, a callback will be arranged; otherwise, your policy will be processed as per terms and conditions. Okay? Okay.

qa_matrix.question_text: In the last 24 months, have you smoked or used any tobacco or nicotine products?
Do you consume alcohol ?
Have you ever been advised by a doctor to reduce or stop alcohol consumption ?
qa_matrix.captured_response: No, ma'am, I don't drink. I've never drunk.

High Blood Pressure

Raised lipids
When was your last blood pressure reading taken?
qa_matrix.captured_response: Ma'am, I don't remember the last blood pressure check like that. ... it happened around... I'll tell you. I'll tell you about my entire medical condition.
qa_matrix.suggested_correction: Customer did not provide a specific date, and based on MER date (March 2026) and customer's diagnosis date (Dec 2024), the last reading was more than 12 months ago.

qa_matrix.question_text: Are you on medical treatment?
Has your medication or dosage changed to improve control of your blood pressure within the last 6 months?
qa_matrix.captured_response: No, ma'am. The doctor gave me Amlong 5mg. But after that, for two-four days, my BP didn't come under control. At the beginning, when I went to the doctor, it was 170/90. Okay. And after three days, I went to the doctor again, it was 140/100. So then he gave me Amlong H. Yes. He said, 'Take this.' It's fine now. ... Yes. No, ma'am, my BP is controlled. I take Amlong 5. My report was fine too.
qa_matrix.suggested_correction: Customer's detailed explanation indicates a change in medication (Amlong H was added/switched), contradicting the 'No' recorded in MER.

qa_matrix.question_text: How did your doctor / nurse describe the result of your last blood pressure reading?
qa_matrix.captured_response: At the beginning, when I went to the doctor, it was 170/90. Okay. And after three days, I went to the doctor again, it was 140/100. ... BP is controlled.

qa_matrix.question_text: When was hypertnsion diagnosed
qa_matrix.captured_response: On 27th December 2024, my BP tablets were started. My BP was diagnosed.

qa_matrix.question_text: Apart from your regular follow ups for this condition are you awaiting further: medical tests or investigations, test results or referral to hospital or surgery?
qa_matrix.suggested_correction: The doctor did not ask this specific question related to hypertension follow-ups.

qa_matrix.suggested_correction: Customer's surgery was Feb 2024. If call is March 2026 (MER date), recovery duration is >1 year, not 1-3 months.

qa_matrix.question_text: Please provide full details of condition including symptoms, treatment and complications if any.

qa_matrix.question_text: Please provide current status and date of last consultation for this condition.
qa_matrix.captured_response: Yes, ma'am, I am fine. ... Yes, ma'am, I went for a follow-up recently. I just came back from the doctor. ... Yes, the doctor said everything is fine.

qa_matrix.question_text: Please confirm medical disclosure in previous policy with TALIC?

critical_issues: The doctor stated the insurer name as 'Tata AIG General' at the start of the call, but the MER document is for 'Tata AIA Life Insurance Company Ltd.'. This is a critical discrepancy in company identification.

There are significant date inconsistencies between the customer's provided medical event dates (Oct/Dec 2024, Feb 2024, Dec 2023) and the MER's implied call date (March 2026) and pre-filled recovery durations/onset dates in the MER. This leads to multiple 'Incorrect' statuses for date-related questions.

The MER incorrectly records 'No' for 'Has your medication or dosage changed to improve control of your blood pressure within the last 6 months?' despite the customer's detailed explanation of a medication change (Amlong H added).

The MER incorrectly records 'No' for 'As a result of this condition, do you still experience any of the following symptoms: lethargy, weight gain or constipation?' when the customer explicitly stated 'Weight gain happened, 2-3 kilos.'

The MER incorrectly records 'No disclosures entered' for 'Is there anything else that you would like to share...' and 'Annual Health Checkup', and 'No' for 'Any abnormal medical findings or pending test results...' and 'Medication or treatment that lasted more than 2 weeks...' despite the customer disclosing Dengue, having had various tests (ECG, MRI, biopsy), and being on long-term medication for hypertension and thyroid, and having had surgery.

Several mandatory questions from the MER were entirely missed by the doctor, including specific follow-up questions for hypertension, questions about Hernia, Haematuria, Proteinuria, and critically, 'Please confirm medical disclosure in previous policy with TALIC?'.
recommendations: The doctor must clearly state the full and correct legal name of the insurance company at the beginning of the call to avoid confusion and ensure compliance with disclosure requirements.

The doctor needs to ensure all components of a clubbed question are explicitly addressed by the customer. For example, when asking about 'high BP, low BP, high cholesterol, or anything?', the doctor should confirm 'No' for cholesterol and lipids if the customer only mentions hypertension.

The doctor should actively listen to the customer's detailed responses and clarify any contradictions. When a customer provides a detailed explanation that contradicts a simple 'Yes/No' answer, the doctor must probe further to capture accurate information, especially regarding medication changes.

All mandatory questions from the MER document must be asked during the conversation. Missing questions like those related to specific hypertension follow-ups, hernia, haematuria, proteinuria, and previous policy disclosures represent significant compliance gaps.

The doctor should ensure that all disclosed medical information, including past tests, treatments, and conditions like Dengue, is accurately captured and reflected in the MER, rather than defaulting to 'No' or 'No disclosures entered' when information was provided.

There appears to be a discrepancy between the MER's recorded call date (March 2026) and the customer's timeline of events (mostly 2024). This should be investigated as it impacts the accuracy of 'within X months/years' type questions. The MER should be pre-filled with accurate dates or updated based on the conversation.

customer_satisfaction_indicators: Customer is cooperative and provides detailed information when prompted.
Customer confirms understanding and agreement at the end of the call.

validation_errors: The MER's stated 'Date 31-March-2026' and 'Dated Time of Medical Verification 02/04/2026' is inconsistent with the customer's medical history dates (e.g., ECG 7th October 2024, BP diagnosed 27th December 2024, surgery 10th February 2024, fistula consultation December 2023). This discrepancy affects the accuracy of time-sensitive questions in the MER.
The doctor stated the insurer name as 'Tata AIG General' (00:00:08, 00:00:11) while the MER document is from 'Tata AIA Life Insurance Company Ltd.', indicating a mismatch in the declared entity.

greetings.explanation: The doctor initiated the call with a greeting.

call_opening.explanation: The doctor introduced herself, confirmed the client's name, and stated the insurer's name.

language_preference.explanation: The doctor did not explicitly ask for the customer's language preference.

id_validation.explanation: The doctor validated the customer's name, date of birth, last four digits of Aadhaar, and city.

disclaimer.explanation: The doctor provided disclaimers regarding the call purpose, legal contract, consequences of incorrect information, and call recording.

politeness.explanation: The doctor maintained a polite and professional tone throughout the call, using respectful language.

empathy.explanation: The doctor showed empathy by asking about the customer's current status and well-being after mentioning recent surgery and ongoing health conditions.

communication_skills.explanation: The doctor provided clear instructions and maintained good clarity in communication.

probing.explanation: The doctor consistently asked follow-up questions to gather detailed information about the customer's medical history and conditions.

observations.explanation: The call experienced a disconnection and reconnection during the initial phase.

call_closure.explanation: The doctor provided a clear declaration of the information and concluded the call politely.
